summary:
Alginate impression materials transition through stages including an initial setting that allows for use until they become rigid; thus, "immediately" refers to just before becoming non-functional due to rigidity loss shortly after achieving flexibility. Sentence Two: Working times are critical because this defines when clinicians can safely manipulate impressions without compromising accuracy or integrity during dental procedures involving such materials.
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning:

Alginate impression materials have a short usable timeframe immediately after acquiring their flexible state but lose functionality rapidly thereafter. Understanding its precise working time ensures accurate manipulation for optimal results in dentistry. To refine further while keeping within constraints,

After attaining elasticity, alginate has only minutes left useful for mold creation; hence defining proper handling windows critically impacts procedural success.
